excerpt: Investing in healthcare stocks can provide stable returns due to the sector's consistent demand for health services and products. Key areas include weight loss, data analytics, robotics, and medical devices. Consult a financial advisor for personalized investment advice.

content.introduction: Investing in healthcare stocks can provide stable returns due to the sector's consistent demand for health services and products. Key areas include weight loss, data analytics, robotics, and medical devices. Consult a financial advisor for personalized investment advice.
body: # Health care sector: Investing in stocks that help keep you well. Regardless of your age, condition, or financial situation, you likely spend some money on products and services to maintain your health, whether it’s a few bucks at the pharmacy or a major hospital visit. This focus on wellness is part of what makes health care a relatively stable part of anyone’s investment portfolio. Knowing which industries in the sector to invest in and when can help you position your portfolio for growth, mitigate market risk, and take advantage of both stable and speculative opportunities in the market. ## What is the health care sector? The health care sector includes a range of companies that offer products and services aimed at maintaining and improving human health. If you’re wary of investing in individual stocks, you can still target the health care sector by investing in a themed mutual fund or exchange-traded fund (ETF).

Some health care ETFs, such as the Health Care SPDR Select Sector Fund, are sector-wide ETFs — basically, health care [index funds](https://www.nerdwallet.com/investing/learn/how-to-invest-in-index-funds) — that include companies from all of the industries discussed above. [1](#1 "Footnote 1") Year-to-date, healthcare returns are modestly above the [S&P 500](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/is-a-market-correction-coming.html), which suggests momentum has improved. AI already supports a range of practical uses, including helping researchers identify promising paths for new medicines, assisting with diagnosis and treatment planning, and analyzing medical images such as X-rays, CT scans, and MRIs. It can also help monitor patients and send real-time alerts to care teams, which may reduce complications and improve follow-through. “Weight loss, data analytics, tools and diagnostics, robotics, medical devices, and insurance are among areas we regard as having the highest investment appeal,” says Terry Sandven, chief equity strategist for U.S. Bank Asset Management Group. That view highlights both the human need at the center of healthcare and the practical reality that better tools and better systems can improve care while supporting stronger business results. Consider consulting with your [financial professional](https://www.usbank.com/wealth-management/find-an-advisor/) to determine how healthcare investments can work within the context of your overall financial plan.
